mission.title: Install and Configure Interlink on Kubernetes
mission.description: InterLink provides an abstraction layer for executing Kubernetes pods on remote resources, enabling the development of provider-specific plugins for the Kubernetes Virtual Kubelet interface. Installing InterLink allows you to leverage its capabilities for managing container execution lifecycles across various environments.

steps.title: Add the InterLink Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the InterLink Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add interlink https://interlink-hq.github.io/interLink/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install InterLink using Helm
steps.description:
Install InterLink in a new namespace called 'interlink' with the following command:
```bash
helm install interlink interlink/interlink --namespace interlink --create-namespace --version 0.6.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the InterLink pods are running successfully with:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace interlink
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for InterLink
steps.description:
Edit the InterLink deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment in your editor:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment interlink --namespace interlink
```
In the editor, add the following under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Set up TLS for secure communication
steps.description:
To enable TLS, you need to create a Kubernetes secret with your TLS certificate and key:
```bash
kubectl create secret tls interlink-tls --cert=/path/to/tls.crt --key=/path/to/tls.key --namespace interlink
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of InterLink will show the pods running in the 'interlink' namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace interlink`. Additionally, you will have configured resource limits and set up TLS for secure communication.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add interlink https://interlink-hq.github.io/interLink/
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the InterLink Helm release from the 'interlink' namespace using the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall interlink --namespace interlink
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with InterLink. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete crd interlinks.interlink.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace interlink
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'interlink' namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace interlink
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your InterLink installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace interlink -o yaml > interlink-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the InterLink installation to the latest version (e.g., 0.7.0):
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade interlink interlink/interlink --namespace interlink --version 0.7.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the InterLink pods are running successfully after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace interlink
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If your InterLink pods are stuck in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the pod to diagnose the issue:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace interlink
```

troubleshooting.title: TLS handshake errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter TLS handshake errors, verify that the TLS secret is correctly created and the certificate and key are valid:
```bash
kubectl get secret interlink-tls --namespace interlink -o yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you see errors related to insufficient resources, check the resource limits set in the deployment and the available resources in your cluster:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment interlink --namespace interlink
kubectl describe nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive an error indicating that the Helm release is not found, ensure that you are using the correct namespace and release name:
```bash
helm list --namespace interlink
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
